description.en: The `/etc/group` file contains a list of all the valid groups defined in the system. The command below allows read/write access for root and read access for everyone else.

rationale.en: The `/etc/group` file needs to be protected from unauthorized changes by non\-privileged users, but needs to be readable as this information is used with many non\-privileged programs.

audit.en:
Run the following commands to remove excess permissions, set owner, and set group on `/etc/group`:

```
# chmod u-x,go-wx /etc/group
# chown root:root /etc/group
```

remediation.en:
Run the following commands to remove excess permissions, set owner, and set group on `/etc/group`:

```
# chmod u-x,go-wx /etc/group
# chown root:root /etc/group
```
